fields.caption: codex can't escalate to root via sudo / if you're already in the docker group

narrative_summary: Riffing on the specific technical detail that Docker group membership grants root-level privileges anyway, the joke frames the 'no sudo' restriction as hilariously pointless security theater — you blocked the front door but left the freight elevator wide open.
rationale: Candidate 4 earns its punchline with the sharpest contrast — 'escalate to root via sudo' vs 'already in the docker group' — making the logical trap explicit and self-contained rather than just restating the setup. The word 'escalate' mirrors real sysadmin/security language, adding specificity, and the framing ('you blocked the front door but left the freight elevator open') is the freshest angle of the five.
story_title: Codex just found a "workaround" of not having sudo on my PC
place: inside a Zoom waiting room for all eternity
hat_pair: foam server rack worn as a hat with blinking LED lights
crash helmet with a tiny working traffic light mounted on top
